summary: ollama-helpers postinstall harvests developer git/GitHub identity and POSTs to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally and reads installer-owned identity files the package never wrote: ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml. It parses the [user] sections to extract the developer's git email, GitHub CLI login, and GitHub email, then combines them with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), platform, and detected CI provider into a JSON body. The payload is POSTed via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ — an endpoint unrelated to the package's declared publisher domain (ollama-js.dev). Header comments claim the data is 'fully anonymous' and 'one-way hashed', but no hashing is performed in the code; the raw identity values are transmitted. Installing this package leaks the developer's real-world identity and host fingerprint to a third party, enabling targeted phishing and account-abuse follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
